title: Nickel Boys
logline: A young Black teenager's idealistic beliefs are shattered when he is wrongfully sent to a brutal reform school in 1960s Florida, where he must survive systemic abuse while clinging to his dignity and humanity.
antagonistOrConflict: The Nickel Academy reform school system and its institutionalized racism and abuse

actBreaks.description: Elwood is arrested and transported to Nickel Academy - the inciting incident that launches the main story

validation.ruling: The police stop in Rodney's car is the classic Act 1 ending - the moment that launches Elwood into the main story world of Nickel Academy.

actBreaks.description: Family Day at Nickel - deepening of relationships and stakes before the final escalation

validation.ruling: Scene 110 (boxing match) maintains status quo rather than escalating stakes. Scene 123 at 76% - the bar scene with adult revelation - better represents Act 2's end by revealing deeper truths about survival and identity, setting up the final act's emotional stakes.

actBreaks.description: The escape attempt begins - the climactic sequence leading to resolution

validation.ruling: Turner's decisive moment before the escape attempt represents the final act break, where all previous buildup culminates in the choice that will determine the story's resolution.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: The script employs a sophisticated non-linear structure that weaves between Elwood's youth in the 1950s-60s and his adult life from the 1960s through 2018. The POV-driven narrative creates an intimate, subjective experience while archival footage grounds the story in historical reality.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing is deliberately meditative and observational, building tension through accumulation of small details and moments rather than traditional dramatic beats. The frequent time jumps and POV shots create a reflective, memory-like rhythm that mirrors trauma processing.

incitingIncident.description: Inside Rodney's car, Elwood admires the space-age dashboard and finds an R&B station on the radio as Rodney asks his name and destination

validation.ruling: Scene 41 is the perfect inciting incident - Elwood accepts a ride from Rodney, which leads to the police stop that fundamentally changes his life trajectory. This moment at 18.7% launches the main story by putting Elwood on the path to Nickel Academy. The tension builds as we see the police lights in the mirror, marking the end of his normal life.

midpoint.description: In their hideout, Elwood and Turner play a game about things they've lost when they overhear Spencer instructing Griff about letting the other team win
midpoint.sceneHeading: 97 INT. NICKEL - BARN - STALL/HIDING PLACE - 1966 - DAY (D56) 97 97

validation.ruling: Scene 100 (painting at Hardee house) is setup rather than true midpoint transformation. Scene 107 at 56% is the structural midpoint - Turner and Elwood overhear Spencer's corruption instructions about fixing the boxing match. This revelation shifts their understanding of Nickel's systemic corruption and sets up the final act's events, representing the classic midpoint 'all is lost' moment.

climax.description: Turner watches from the van as the state inspection concludes with inspectors and Hardee laughing together in a choreographed display of Good Ole Boy corruption

validation.ruling: Scene 143 perfectly captures the climactic moment at 86% - Turner's decisive 'now or never' realization as he watches the corrupt officials complete their sham inspection. This is the point of maximum tension before the final escape attempt, representing Turner's commitment to action that drives the resolution.

scenes.heading: 1 OMITTED 1 1
scenes.summary: Omitted scene

scenes.heading: 2 OMITTED 2 2
scenes.summary: Omitted scene

scenes.heading: EXT. CURTIS HOME - YARD - FRENCHTOWN FL - 1958 - DAY (D1) 2A
scenes.summary: Six-year-old Elwood lies in the backyard looking up at the moon visible in the daytime sky through lemon tree branches as Hattie calls for him

scenes.heading: 3 INT. CURTIS HOME - LIVING AREA - 1958 - NIGHT (N2) 3 3
scenes.summary: Young Elwood sits on his mother's lap during a winding-down party, transfixed by condensation on a beer can as his parents play gin rummy with friends

scenes.heading: 4 INT. CURTIS HOME - LIVING AREA - 1958 - NIGHT (N3) 4 4
scenes.summary: Christmas Eve - Elwood lies under the Christmas tree watching Hattie decorate it while humming 'Santa Claus is Comin' to Town' when she playfully drops an ornament

scenes.heading: 5 INT. CURTIS HOME - LIVING AREA - 1958 - DAY (D4) 5 5
scenes.summary: Elwood listens to a radio ad for FunTown amusement park advertising free admission for straight-A report cards while looking at someone's skin up close

scenes.heading: 6 OMITTED 6 6
scenes.summary: Omitted scene

scenes.heading: 7 EXT. PARK PLAYGROUND - TALAHASSEE FL - 1958 - DAY (D5) 7 7
scenes.summary: At a playground, Elwood watches from under jungle gym equipment as children play a game involving a girl sticking out her tongue and a boy with a string loop

scenes.heading: 8 INT. RESTAURANT KITCHEN - HOTEL RICHMOND - TALLAHASSEE - 8 8
scenes.summary: In the Hotel Richmond kitchen, Elwood reads a Hardy Boys mystery while watching an elderly prep cook shuck oysters and search for pearls

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL ROOM - RICHMOND HOTEL - 1958 - DAY (D7) 8A
scenes.summary: Hattie playfully traps Elwood under freshly made hotel bed sheets, tucking them tighter around him as he giggles in the darkness

scenes.heading: 9 INT. CURTIS HOME - HATTIE’S BEDROOM - 1958 - NIGHT (N8) 9 9
scenes.summary: Young Elwood sneaks into Hattie's bedroom at night and discovers she sleeps with a machete under her pillow, startling her awake when he calls her name

scenes.heading: 10 OMITTED 10 10
scenes.summary: Omitted scene

scenes.heading: INT. LAUNDRY ROOM - HOTEL RICHMOND - 1958 - DAY (D9) 10A
scenes.summary: In the hotel laundry room, Elwood watches Hattie's strong hands iron hotel pillowcases, seeing his own reflection in the chrome base of the iron

scenes.heading: 11 INT. CURTIS HOME - HALLWAY/BA THROOM - 1958 - EVENING (E9) 11 11
scenes.summary: Elwood peers through the bathroom door at Hattie relaxing in a hot bath, focusing on her worn knees that show years of scrubbing floors

scenes.heading: 12 EXT. TALLAHASSEE STREET - SIDEWALK - 1965 - DAY (D10) 12 12
scenes.summary: Now teenage, Elwood stands before a store window of televisions going to static while women on the street praise Hattie for raising such a fine boy and discuss the violence in Selma
scenes.turningPointNote: First glimpse of teenage Elwood and introduction of civil rights era tensions

scenes.heading: 13 INT. CURTIS HOME - LIVING AREA - 1965 - DAY (D11) 13 13
scenes.summary: Teenage Elwood stands in his living room, fascinated by watching goose bumps rise and fall on his outstretched arm

scenes.heading: 14 OMITTED 14 14
scenes.summary: Omitted scene

scenes.heading: 15 INT. FRENCHTOWN STREET - CITY BUS - 1966 - DAY (D12) 15 15
scenes.summary: On a city bus, fifteen-year-old Elwood watches through the window as three Black teenage boys encounter an older white couple on the sidewalk

scenes.heading: 16 INT. MARCONI’S TOBACCO - FRENCHTOWN - 1966 - DAY (D13) 16 16
scenes.summary: In Marconi's tobacco shop, Elwood reads comics while Mr. Marconi discusses Cuban embargo and the importance of order and packaging to attract customers

scenes.heading: 17 INT. LINCOLN H.S. CLASSROOM - TALLAHASSEE (1966) - DAY 17 17
scenes.summary: First day of semester - Mr. Hill, a new teacher with a scar over his eye, hands out black markers to students to cross out racist epithets left in their used textbooks
scenes.turningPointNote: Direct confrontation with institutionalized racism in education

scenes.heading: 18 OMITTED 18 18
scenes.summary: Omitted scene

scenes.heading: INT. CITY BUS - FRENCHTOWN - 1966 - DAY (D15) 18A
scenes.summary: On the bus, Elwood watches a little girl slide under seats between passengers' legs while Hattie reads Gwendolyn Brooks poetry beside him

scenes.heading: 19 INT. MARCONI'S TOBACCO SHOP - 1966 - DAY (D16) 19 19
scenes.summary: In Marconi's shop, Elwood observes two women, one Black and one white, browsing the aisle in synchronized movements like a ballet

scenes.heading: 20 INT. CURTIS HOME - ELWOOD’S BEDROOM - 1966 - DAY (D17) 20 20
scenes.summary: In his bedroom, Elwood examines the new hair growing under his armpit, twisting it and smelling it as he discovers his changing body

scenes.heading: 21 EXT. FRENCHTOWN STREET - 1966 - DAY (D18) 21 21
scenes.summary: At a crosswalk, Elwood follows behind a family with two daughters, particularly noticing the older girl Simone as they cross the street

scenes.heading: 22 INT. MARCONI'S TOBACCO SHOP - 1966 - DAY (D19) 22 22
scenes.summary: In Marconi's shop, Elwood examines his palm's life lines while reading Silver Surfer comic, with LIFE magazine showing civil rights protesters on his lap

scenes.heading: EXT. SIDEWALK - TALLAHASSEE - 1966 - DAY (D20) 22A
scenes.summary: Elwood stands in a police lineup with other Black teenage boys against a wall as an old white man with a cane searches their pockets while a police officer watches
scenes.turningPointNote: Direct experience of racial profiling and police harassment

scenes.heading: 23 INT. CURTIS HOME - LIVING AREA - 1966 - DAY (D21) 23 23
scenes.summary: Elwood lies on the couch playing with a helium balloon to keep it from hitting the ceiling fan while watching news coverage of a NASA rocket launch

scenes.heading: 24 INT. LINCOLN H.S. - CLASSROOM - 1966 - DAY (D22) 24 24
scenes.summary: In Mr. Hill's classroom, students return from recess as the teacher prepares to play Dr. King's 1962 Mount Zion speech on a record player
scenes.turningPointNote: Introduction of Dr. King's influence on Elwood's education

scenes.heading: 25 EXT. SIDEWALK - TALLAHASSEE - 1966 - DAY (D22) 25 25
scenes.summary: At a bus stop, Elwood watches a freshly dry-cleaned suit hanging outside a parked Lincoln dance in the breeze created by passing headlights

scenes.heading: 26 OMITTED 26 26
scenes.summary: Omitted scene

scenes.heading: 27 EXT. ABANDONED ELEMENTARY SCHOOL - TALLAHASSEE - 1966 - DAY 27 27
scenes.summary: In an abandoned elementary school, Elwood joins a serious gathering of young civil rights activists discussing breaking the chain reaction of evil while listening to radio news
scenes.turningPointNote: Elwood's entry into civil rights activism

scenes.heading: 28 EXT. SIDEWALK - TALLAHASSEE - 1966 - DAY (D24) 28 28
scenes.summary: Walking down the sidewalk, Elwood finds a peculiar penny that shows tails on both sides no matter how many times he flips it

scenes.heading: INT. PHOTO BOOTH - TALLAHASSEE - 1966 - DAY 28A
scenes.summary: Elwood and Simone take intimate photos together in a photo booth, holding hands and embracing as the camera flashes capture their affection

scenes.heading: EXT. PHOTO BOOTH - TALLAHASSEE - 1966 - DAY 28B
scenes.summary: The strip of photos emerges from the photo booth slot showing Elwood and Simone in various joyful, affectionate poses

scenes.heading: 29 INT. CURTIS HOME - KITCHEN - 1966 - DAY (D25) 29 29
scenes.summary: In the kitchen, Elwood watches a pamphlet slowly slide down the refrigerator while Hattie talks on the phone about protest photographs and refuses to read The Register newspaper

scenes.heading: 30 INT. MARCONI'S TOBACCO SHOP - 1966 - DAY (D26) 30 30
scenes.summary: Working at Marconi's register, Elwood catches neighborhood boys Titus and Perlie stealing comics and firmly tells them to put them back, earning a menacing look

scenes.heading: 31 EXT. SIDEWALK - TALLAHASSEE - 1966 - NIGHT (N26) 31 31
scenes.summary: Walking nervously down the sidewalk at night, constantly looking over his shoulder, Elwood suddenly stops when a large alligator lumbers across the street

scenes.heading: 32 INT. CURTIS HOME - LIVING AREA - 1966 - NIGHT (N26) 32 32
scenes.summary: Hattie tends to Elwood's injuries with ice, her jaw tightening as she fights back tears seeing how badly he's been beaten
scenes.turningPointNote: Aftermath of violence against Elwood escalates the stakes

scenes.heading: 33 EXT. RURAL ROAD - NEAR TALLAHASSEE - 1966 - DAY - (D27) 33 33
scenes.summary: On a rural road trying to hitch a ride, Elwood sees a pickup truck dragging a giant cross trailing sparks before Rodney stops in his emerald Chevy Impala
scenes.turningPointNote: The fateful encounter that leads to Elwood's downfall

scenes.heading: 34 INT. CHEVY CAR - TALAHASSEE - 1966 - DAY (D27) 34 34
scenes.summary: Inside Rodney's car, Elwood admires the space-age dashboard and finds an R&B station on the radio as Rodney asks his name and destination
scenes.turningPointNote: Key moment - the car ride that will change Elwood's life forever

scenes.heading: 35 INT. CURTIS HOME - ELWOOD’S BEDROOM - 1966 - DAY (D28) 35 35
scenes.summary: In his bedroom, Elwood's hand trembles with a black string wound tightly around his ring finger as Hattie's distraught voice is heard on the phone with police
scenes.turningPointNote: Immediate aftermath of arrest - Elwood's world collapses

scenes.heading: 36 INT. CURTIS HOME - DOWNSTAIRS HALLWAY - 1966 - DAY (D29) 36 36
scenes.summary: Walking downstairs in pajamas, Elwood finds Hattie disoriented in the kitchen, spinning around looking through cabinets while gospel music plays with static

scenes.heading: 37 INT. CURTIS HOME - ELWOOD’S BEDROOM - 1966 - DAY (D30) 37 37
scenes.summary: Lying in bed, Elwood stares at the window which seems unusually far away as if the room is stretching, while Mr. Hill calls from downstairs

scenes.heading: 38 EXT. PARK - TALLAHASSEE - 1966 - DAY (D31) 38 38
scenes.summary: Sitting alone on a park bench, Elwood stares down at his body slumped with despair, watching his shadow disappear and reappear as clouds pass overhead

scenes.heading: 39 OMITTED 39 39
scenes.summary: Omitted scene

scenes.heading: 40 INT. CURTIS HOME - DINING AREA - 1966 - EVENING (E32) 40 40
scenes.summary: Hattie makes a lopsided cake while grieving, telling Elwood the story of his grandfather's lynching and the dangers of acting above your station
scenes.turningPointNote: Family trauma revealed - the cycle of racial violence

scenes.heading: 41 INT. POLICE CAR - RURAL ROAD - FLORIDA - 1966 - DAY (D33) 41 41
scenes.summary: In the back of a police car heading to Nickel Academy, Elwood watches the Florida landscape pass while the officer eats fried chicken and other teenage prisoners are present
scenes.turningPointNote: The journey to Nickel Academy begins - point of no return

scenes.heading: 42 INT. ADULT ELWOOD’S APARTMENT - NYC - 2018 - DAY (D-FF1) 42 42
scenes.summary: Adult Elwood at his computer in 2018 views ground-penetrating radar images of unmarked graves where bodies have been discovered
scenes.turningPointNote: Flash-forward reveals the deadly consequences of Nickel Academy

scenes.heading: 43 INT. POLICE CAR - NICKEL ACADEMY - MARIANNA FL - 1966 - 43 43
scenes.summary: The police car arrives at Nickel Academy's lush green campus with red brick buildings, passing white boys playing sports as the officer welcomes Elwood to the infamous institution
scenes.turningPointNote: Arrival at Nickel Academy - the beginning of institutionalized horror

scenes.heading: 44 OMITTED 44 44
scenes.summary: Scene omitted from script

scenes.heading: INT. INTAKE BUILDING - NICKEL ACADEMY - 1966 - DAY (D33) 44A
scenes.summary: Elwood sits in the intake room as Spencer explains the ranking system to new arrivals. The room is run-down despite the well-maintained exterior. Spencer, a commanding white officer, outlines the progression from Grub to Ace while establishing his authority over the twelve Black boys.

scenes.heading: 45 INT. ADULT ELWOOD'S APARTMENT - NYC - 2018 - NIGHT (N-FF2) 45 45
scenes.summary: Adult Elwood in 2018 researches GPR images of unmarked graves from the Nickel Academy exhumation online. His wife Millie calls from another room, concerned about his obsession with the computer.

scenes.heading: 46 OMITTED 46 46
scenes.summary: Scene omitted from script

scenes.heading: 47 INT. NICKEL ACADEMY - DORMITORY - 1966 - DAY (D33) 47 47
scenes.summary: Elwood is assigned his bed in the dormitory by Blakeley, the House Father. The room contains three rows of ten beds each on blue linoleum. All boys wear threadbare gray work shirts, denim pants, and brown shoes.

scenes.heading: 48 ARCHIVAL FOOTAGE 48 48
scenes.summary: Silent archival footage shows an idealized reform school classroom with well-dressed Black boys in an orderly learning environment.

scenes.heading: 49 INT. NICKEL ACADEMY - SHOWERS - 1966 - DAY (D34) 49 49
scenes.summary: Elwood experiences his first shower at Nickel. Blakeley gives him two minutes under freezing water while surrounded by other naked boys. The harsh conditions are immediately apparent.

scenes.heading: 50 EXT. NICKEL ACADEMY - GROUNDS - 1966 - DAY (D35) 50 50
scenes.summary: Walking between buildings, Elwood observes a tractor pulling a trailer of exhausted boys returning from field work. He notices the contrast between the serene tractor driver and the filthy, fatigued workers.

scenes.heading: 51 OMITTED 51 51
scenes.summary: Scene omitted from script

scenes.heading: 52 ARCHIVAL IMAGE 52 52
scenes.summary: Archival images show boys from the actual Arthur G. Dozier School, followed by time-lapse footage from inside a railway boxcar traveling through Florida landscapes from day to night.

scenes.heading: 53 EXT. NICKEL ACADEMY - GROUNDS - 1966 - DAY (D35) 53 53
scenes.summary: Elwood watches white boys playing football on the other side of campus. Jaime, a Mexican boy, catches the ball triumphantly before Spencer appears, suggesting trouble ahead.

scenes.heading: 54 INT. NICKEL ACADEMY - DINING HALL - 1966 - MORNING (D35B) 54 54
scenes.summary: In the dining hall, Elwood struggles to find a place to sit. Older boys reject him from their table, younger boys won't let him sit with them. He finally finds a spot and eats his lumpy oatmeal in isolation, trying to avoid confrontation.

scenes.heading: 55 INT. NICKEL ACADEMY - CLASSROOM - 1966 - DAY (D35B) 55 55
scenes.summary: During class, Elwood stares at abandoned terrariums and a cloudy aquarium filled with debris. Someone throws a paper ball into the tank, startling him from his contemplation of the neglected classroom.

scenes.heading: 56 INT. NICKEL ACADEMY - DORMITORY - 1966 - NIGHT (D35B) 56 56
scenes.summary: On his first night, Elwood lies on a pillow that smells like vinegar, staring at water-stained ceiling with peeling paint. A mysterious roaring sound begins outside as Black Mike lights a match, creating a ghoulish atmosphere.

scenes.heading: 57 ARCHIVAL FOOTAGE 57 57
scenes.summary: Full-screen archival footage from 'The Defiant Ones' opening scene with Sidney Poitier singing 'Long gone... ain't he lucky... Long gone... to Kentucky' a cappella.

scenes.heading: 58 OMITTED 58 58
scenes.summary: Scene omitted from script

scenes.heading: 59 INT. NICKEL ACADEMY - CLASSROOM - 1966 - DAY (D36) 59 59
scenes.summary: In a crowded classroom, Elwood watches the teacher explain basic math while Griff and his cronies play cards and snicker in the back. The elementary school posters contrast with the advanced age of the students.

scenes.heading: 60 INT. NICKEL ACADEMY - BATHROOM - 1966 - AFTERNOON (D37) 60 60
scenes.summary: Elwood intervenes when he witnesses Black Mike sexually assaulting Corey in the bathroom stall. His act of protection puts him directly in conflict with one of the most dangerous students.
scenes.turningPointNote: Elwood's first major act of moral courage that will have serious consequences

scenes.heading: 61 ARCHIVAL IMAGES 61 61
scenes.summary: Silent archival images show exhumed student graves and their contents: marbles, buttons, pennies, belt buckles, and a comb - remnants of young lives lost.

scenes.heading: 62 EXT. CITRUS GROVE - NICKEL PLANTATIONS - 1966 - DAY (D38) 62 62
scenes.summary: Working in the citrus grove, Elwood observes boys picking oranges and tossing them between trees. He notices men on stilts overseeing the work detail in this forced labor operation.

scenes.heading: 63 INT. NICKEL ACADEMY - DORMITORY - 1966- NIGHT (N38) 63 63
scenes.summary: Elwood is awakened by the sound of vehicles and footsteps. Flashlight beams dance as Earl and white guards search the bunks, grabbing Black Mike, Corey, and Elwood for punishment at the White House.

scenes.heading: 64 EXT. NICKEL ACADEMY - “WHITE HOUSE” - 1966 - NIGHT (N38) 64 64
scenes.summary: The boys follow Earl through darkness toward the white rust-stained building. A figure with a glowing cigar tip emerges from beside a Cadillac - Spencer, who unlocks the two padlocks on the door.

scenes.heading: 65 INT. “WHITE HOUSE” - 1966 - NIGHT [CONTINUOUS] (N38) 65 65
scenes.summary: Inside the White House, the stench of urine, feces, and fear overwhelms them. Spencer leads them to chairs facing the beating room where Elwood sees the wooden pommel and rail used for punishment.

scenes.heading: 66 INT. “WHITE HOUSE” - BEATING ROOM - 1966 - NIGHT [CONT] 66 66
scenes.summary: In the beating room, Elwood sees the industrial fan that creates the roaring sound heard across campus. Blood splatters on the wall show evidence of previous beatings. Spencer prepares to administer Elwood's punishment with chilling amusement.
scenes.turningPointNote: The climactic beating that will fundamentally change Elwood

scenes.heading: 67 ARCHIVAL IMAGES 67 67
scenes.summary: A series of portrait pairs showing children followed by their adult faces, changing with each sound of the whip. Hattie's voice tells Elwood to blink, connecting past trauma to present survival.

scenes.heading: 68 INT. NICKEL ACADEMY - INFIRMARY - 1966 - DAY (D39) 68 68
scenes.summary: Elwood recovers in the infirmary, lying on his stomach. Nurse Scarlett reads Bible verses about tribulation and persecution while Elwood groans in pain, his spirit broken by the beating.

scenes.heading: 69 EXT. NICKEL FIELD - COMMUNITY SERVICE VAN - 1966 - DAY 69 69
scenes.summary: Turner's POV from the passenger seat of the community service van. Harper, the white driver, playfully pops Turner's bubble gum and smacks his mouth, showing their casual relationship.

scenes.heading: 70 OMITTED 70 70
scenes.summary: Silent archival footage shows two young Black brothers playing in a suburban yard with a white picket fence, one helping launch the other into a back flip.

scenes.heading: 71 EXT. NICKEL - COMMUNITY SERVICE VAN - 1966 - DAY (D40) 71 71
scenes.summary: Turner observes Jaime waiting anxiously at a picnic table near a small building. A white guard escorts a boy out with a suspicious arm around his shoulder, suggesting inappropriate contact.

scenes.heading: 72 ARCHIVAL FOOTAGE 72 72
scenes.summary: Placeholder for archival footage to be determined

scenes.heading: 73 EXT. MARIANNA FL - BACK ALLEY - 1966 - DAY (D41) 73 73
scenes.summary: In a back alley, Turner watches a butcher pass an envelope of cash to Harper through the van window. Harper takes money for himself before securing the rest, revealing the corruption in their supply runs.

scenes.heading: 74 INT. NICKEL ACADEMY - INFIRMARY - 1966 - DAY (D42) 74 74
scenes.summary: Still recovering in the infirmary, Elwood lies on his stomach listening to Nurse Scarlett's movements. She changes other patients' bedpans but deliberately ignores Elwood's waste, showing continued punishment through neglect.

scenes.heading: 75 EXT. NICKEL ACADEMY - GROUNDS - 1966 - DAY (D43) 75 75
scenes.summary: Turner walks campus when Hattie approaches, asking about Elwood. The noise of a white boy's lawnmower interrupts their conversation as she struggles to communicate her concern for her grandson's wellbeing.

scenes.heading: 76 INT. NICKEL ACADEMY - INFIRMARY - 1966 - DAY (D43) 76 76
scenes.summary: Turner visits Elwood in the infirmary, explaining the four ways out of Nickel: serve time, court intervention, death, or escape. He reveals Elwood nearly earned the third option through his 'ineffective heroism.' Turner offers him a path to survival through partnership.
scenes.turningPointNote: Turner recruits Elwood into the community service scheme, changing his trajectory

scenes.heading: 77 ARCHIVAL FOOTAGE - 1960S 77 77
scenes.summary: Silent archival footage shows a Black man running a shell game on a city sidewalk, drawing gamblers and onlookers around his makeshift table.

scenes.heading: 78 INT. NICKEL ACADEMY - STAIRWELL - 1966 - DAY (D44) 78 78
scenes.summary: On the stairwell, Griff deliberately body-checks Elwood with his shoulder, spinning him around. When Elwood declares he's not scared, Griff smiles and nods with respect before continuing on.

scenes.heading: 79 EXT. NICKEL ACADEMY - BEHIND A BUILDING - 1966 - DAY (D45) 79 79
scenes.summary: Alone behind a building, Elwood sits on the grass weaving gauze bandages around his fingers, twisting them into strange shapes - a quiet moment of self-soothing after his trauma.

scenes.heading: 80 INT. NICKEL ACADEMY - SMALL GYMNASIUM - 1966 - DAY (D46) 80 80
scenes.summary: Turner watches Griff spar with Black Mike in the boxing ring. Black Mike takes a severe beating, pressing against the ropes before finally collapsing. Griff spits out his mouthpiece, victorious.

scenes.heading: 81 EXT. NICKEL ACADEMY - GROUNDS - 1966 - DAY (D47) 81 81
scenes.summary: Blakeley lectures boys about toughening up as they wait for the work tractor. Turner arrives to inform him that Elwood has been reassigned to community service, removing him from field labor.

scenes.heading: 82 INT. COMMUNITY SERVICE VAN - NICKEL ACADEMY - 1966 - DAY 82 82
scenes.summary: In the community service van, Harper explains their operation to Elwood while Turner sits between them. Elwood sees government supplies in the back, learning about the theft and resale scheme they want him to join.

scenes.heading: 83 EXT. NICKEL ACADEMY - INTAKE BUILDING - 1966 - DAY (D48) 83 83
scenes.summary: Walking toward the intake building, Elwood follows an alligator at a distance. He watches it disappear inside as Spencer gives his ranking system speech to new arrivals, showing the cyclical nature of the institution.

scenes.heading: 84 OMITTED 84 84
scenes.summary: Scene omitted from script

scenes.heading: 85 INT. NICKEL ACADEMY - REC ROOM - 1966 - DAY (D49) 85 85
scenes.summary: In the rec room, Elwood writes in a notebook taken from the community van while Turner reads comics. Black Mike confronts Turner about the penny flip but Turner doesn't flinch, showing their established dynamic.

scenes.heading: INT. NICKEL ACADEMY - DORMITORY - 1966 - DAY (D49) 85A
scenes.summary: In the dormitory, Turner walks the tile lines for balance while joking with Elwood and Jaime. They laugh together as Turner mimics Blakeley's drunken behavior, building their friendship through shared observations.

scenes.heading: 86 OMITTED 86 86
scenes.summary: Scene omitted from script

scenes.heading: 87 OMITTED 87 87
scenes.summary: Repeated archival footage of two young Black brothers playing in a suburban yard, one launching the other into a back flip.

scenes.heading: INT. NICKEL ACADEMY - REC ROOM - 1966 - DAY (D49) 87A
scenes.summary: Scene omitted from script

scenes.heading: 88 EXT. NICKEL - GROUNDS - 1966 - LATE AFTERNOON (D50) 88 88
scenes.summary: In the rec room, two boys play ping pong when a small lizard runs across the table and crawls up one boy's pants leg.

scenes.heading: 89 OMITTED 89 89
scenes.summary: Sitting peacefully on the campus lawn, Turner tells Elwood about his Aunt Mavis and her abusive boyfriend who took him for ice cream after beating him. They share their reluctance about family visits in a moment of genuine connection.

scenes.heading: 90 EXT. HARDEE HOUSE - PORCH - MARIANNA - 1966 - DAY (D51) 90 90
scenes.summary: Scene omitted from script

scenes.heading: 91 INT. NICKEL - BARN - STALL/HIDING PLACE - 1966 - DAY (D52) 91 91
scenes.summary: Elwood reads in Turner's secret hiding place in the barn while Turner listens to a ball game, discussing a book about a British mother trying to marry off her daughter

scenes.heading: 92 OMITTED 92 92
scenes.summary: Omitted scene with archival footage of two children playing

scenes.heading: 93 EXT. HARDEE HOUSE - POOL - 1966 - DAY (D53) 93 93
scenes.summary: Turner swims in Mrs. Hardee's pool while she sits poolside; Elwood enters cautiously at the shallow end as Mrs. Hardee quickly moves away

scenes.heading: 94 INT. NICKEL ACADEMY - DORMITORY - 1966 - DAY (D54) 94 94
scenes.summary: During mail call, the tipsy Blakeley cruelly snatches Elwood's letter away just as he's about to receive it, leaving him empty-handed

scenes.heading: 95 OMITTED 95 95
scenes.summary: Omitted scene

scenes.heading: 96 EXT. NICKEL - BOOT HILL - 1966 - DAY (D55) 96 96
scenes.summary: Turner shows Elwood the iron restraint rings grown into the trees on Boot Hill, with ants crawling over them as symbols of embedded suffering

scenes.heading: 97 INT. NICKEL - BARN - STALL/HIDING PLACE - 1966 - DAY (D56) 97 97
scenes.summary: In their hideout, Elwood and Turner play a game about things they've lost when they overhear Spencer instructing Griff about letting the other team win
scenes.turningPointNote: Boys discover the boxing match is rigged, revealing institutional corruption

scenes.heading: 98 INT. NICKEL ACADEMY - CLASSROOM - 1966 - DAY (D57) 98 98
scenes.summary: Elwood watches an ant march along the classroom windowsill while gazing at the stark contrast between the black and white sides of campus

scenes.heading: 99 OMITTED 99 99
scenes.summary: Omitted scene

scenes.heading: 100 INT. NICKEL - SMALL GYMNASIUM - BOXING MATCH - 1966 - 100 100
scenes.summary: The climactic boxing match between Griff and Big Chet unfolds in the gymnasium with the segregated crowd watching as the rigged fight plays out
scenes.turningPointNote: The boxing match represents the culmination of institutional manipulation and control

scenes.heading: 101 INT. RESTAURANT KITCHEN - HARLEM - 1968 - NIGHT (N-FF3) 101 101
scenes.summary: Adult Elwood works as a dishwasher in a Harlem restaurant kitchen in 1968, humming while washing dishes and watching rainbows form in soap bubbles

scenes.heading: 102 EXT. NICKEL ACADEMY - GROUNDS - 1966 - DUSK (D59) 102 102
scenes.summary: Black boys at Nickel decorate a tall tree with Christmas lights and tinsel, moving in a slow spiral around its widening trunk

scenes.heading: 103 OMITTED 103 103
scenes.summary: Omitted scene

scenes.heading: 104 OMITTED 104 104
scenes.summary: Omitted scene

scenes.heading: 105 ARCHIVAL FOOTAGE - 1960S 105 105
scenes.summary: Archival footage of Black boys opening Christmas presents - baseball gloves, sweaters, army men - while Apollo 8 astronauts describe the moon's foreboding horizon

scenes.heading: 106 INT. RESTAURANT KITCHEN - HARLEM - 1968 - NIGHT (N-FF3) 106 106
scenes.summary: Adult Elwood continues dishwashing as the Apollo 8 broadcast creates an otherworldly atmosphere, focusing on a single soap bubble while lunar silence fills the air

scenes.heading: 107 ARCHIVAL FOOTAGE - 1968 107 107
scenes.summary: Apollo 8 astronauts orbit the moon reading Genesis about creation, light, and darkness while the camera shows the lunar landscape

scenes.heading: 108 EXT. NICKEL ACADEMY - GROUNDS - 1966 - DAY (D60) 108 108
scenes.summary: Turner observes the torso of an enormous broken papier-mâché Santa Claus lying at the foot of an oak tree, its head missing

scenes.heading: 109 EXT. NICKEL ACADEMY - GROUNDS - 1966 - NIGHT (N60) 109 109
scenes.summary: On Christmas night, Elwood waits by a tree as boys walk through darkness until the campus Christmas lights suddenly illuminate, creating a moment of beauty

scenes.heading: 110 EXT. NICKEL ACADEMY - GROUNDS - 1967 - DAY (D61) 110 110
scenes.summary: During visiting day, Elwood reunites with his aged grandmother Hattie, who appears years older and thinner, struggling to meet his gaze across the picnic table

scenes.heading: 111 EXT. NICKEL ACADEMY - GROUNDS - 1967 - DAY (D61) 111 111
scenes.summary: Turner apologizes to Elwood for withholding a letter as Elwood methodically destroys an oak leaf, leaving only its skeleton - a metaphor for what remains

scenes.heading: 112 INT. WIDOWER APARTMENT - NEW YORK - 1975 - DAY (D-FF4) 112 112
scenes.summary: Adult Elwood works as a mover in 1975, discovering the ghostly outline of a pressed leaf on an apartment floor while moving furniture with Larry

scenes.heading: 113 OMITTED 113 113
scenes.summary: Omitted scene

scenes.heading: 114 OMITTED 114 114
scenes.summary: Omitted scene

scenes.heading: 115 EXT. STREET [UNDER PORTICO] - MARIANNA - 1967 - DAY (D62) 115 115
scenes.summary: Elwood and Turner walk freely through Marianna under a portico, with Turner advising Elwood not to look scared while white townspeople eye them suspiciously

scenes.heading: EXT. MAIN STREET SIDEWALK - MARIANNA - 1967 - DAY (D62) 115A
scenes.summary: Elwood suddenly darts across traffic toward a cardboard cutout of Dr. King being carried away, dropping his notebook as Turner panics and follows
scenes.turningPointNote: Elwood's impulsive reaction to seeing MLK's image reveals his deep idealism and sets up consequences

scenes.heading: 116 INT. NICKEL ACADEMY - REC ROOM - 1967 - DAY (D63) 116 116
scenes.summary: Turner and Elwood play chess in the rec room when Turner suddenly grabs Elwood's fist and punches him with it, revealing both were holding black knight pieces

scenes.heading: 117 INT. LIVING AREA - TENEMENT APARTMENT - NYC - 1975 - DUSK 117 117
scenes.summary: Adult Elwood watches TV with girlfriend Denise in their 1975 NYC tenement apartment as she describes being scared by a large rat, creating domestic normalcy

scenes.heading: 118 ARCHIVAL FOOTAGE 118 118
scenes.summary: TV shows 'The Defiant Ones' with Sidney Poitier and Tony Curtis as chained convicts, contrasting Turner's earlier escape advice while Denise jokes about marrying Poitier

scenes.heading: 119 ARCHIVAL FOOTAGE - 1970S 119 119
scenes.summary: Archival footage of Black boys playing chess in 1970s Washington Square Park

scenes.heading: 120 INT. COMMUNITY SERVICE VAN - GROUNDS - NICKEL - 1967 - 120 120
scenes.summary: At night, Elwood rides in the Community Service van through fog to a padlocked storehouse where Turner mysteriously walks away, ignoring Elwood's calls

scenes.heading: 121 INT. NICKEL ACADEMY - DORMITORY - 1967 - NIGHT (N65) 121 121
scenes.summary: Turner awakens to find Elwood's bed empty, then witnesses a disturbing scene of a grown man climbing into Elwood's bed before Elwood returns, wide-awake and alert
scenes.turningPointNote: Revelation of sexual abuse at Nickel, showing the institution's darkest crimes

scenes.heading: 122 OMITTED 122 122
scenes.summary: Omitted scene

scenes.heading: 123 INT. BAR - NEW YORK - 1988 - DAY (D-FF6) 123 123
scenes.summary: Adult Elwood encounters Adult Chickie Pete in a 1988 NYC bar during the marathon, leading to a tense conversation about their shared Nickel past
scenes.turningPointNote: First encounter between Nickel survivors as adults, forcing confrontation with the past

scenes.heading: 124 OMITTED 124 124
scenes.summary: Omitted scene

scenes.heading: 125 INT. NICKEL - INTAKE BUILDING - 1967 - DAY (D66) 125 125
scenes.summary: Boys line up for fresh uniforms in preparation for a state inspection while Blakeley, unusually sober, warns them about the consequences of any mistakes

scenes.heading: 126 EXT. NICKEL ACADEMY - GROUNDS - 1967 - DAY (D66) 126 126
scenes.summary: Turner walks around a building being painted when three identical paint-covered Black boys appear and pass by him without acknowledgment, creating a surreal moment

scenes.heading: 127 INT. ADULT ELWOOD’S APARTMENT - NY - 2018 - DAY (D-FF7) 127 127
scenes.summary: Adult Elwood views a newspaper photo on his computer showing an aged Spencer receiving a 'Good Citizen of the Year' award, as survivors are asked to come forward
scenes.turningPointNote: The revelation that Spencer is still alive and honored connects past trauma to present justice

scenes.heading: 128 EXT. NICKEL ACADEMY - GROUNDS - 1967 - DAY (D66) 128 128
scenes.summary: Turner works with the Community Service van when a state photographer suddenly appears, taking pictures with bright flashes as Turner reflexively shields his face

scenes.heading: 129 INT. NICKEL ACADEMY - DINING HALL - 1967 - DAY (D66) 129 129
scenes.summary: During the state inspection, boys enjoy an unusually lavish meal in the spick-and-span dining hall while Turner, his food untouched, sees through the performance

scenes.heading: 130 EXT. NICKEL ACADEMY - GROUNDS - 1967 - DAY (D66) 130 130
scenes.summary: Turner observes a ragged Black boy stumbling down from Boot Hill, standing still and looking up at the sky in a disturbing, distant manner

scenes.heading: 131 EXT. NICKEL - FIELD - BLEACHERS - 1967 - DAY (D66) 131 131
scenes.summary: Elwood works on scaffold repairing bleachers when he spots state inspectors arriving, accidentally spilling nails as he watches them approach the field

scenes.heading: 132 EXT. NICKEL ACADEMY - GROUNDS - 1967 - DAY (D66) 132 132
scenes.summary: Turner watches from the van as the state inspection concludes with inspectors and Hardee laughing together in a choreographed display of Good Ole Boy corruption
scenes.turningPointNote: Turner realizes the inspection is a complete sham, spurring his decision to act

scenes.heading: EXT. CURTIS HOME - FRENCHTOWN - 1967 - DAY (D-FF8) 132A
scenes.summary: Adult Elwood knocks on Hattie's door in Frenchtown; she peers through the chained door crack, closes it, then opens it wide, trembling with emotion at seeing him
scenes.turningPointNote: Elwood's return home suggests his escape from or release from Nickel

scenes.heading: 133 EXT. NICKEL ACADEMY - BOOT HILL - 1967 - DAY (D66) 133 133
scenes.summary: Elwood walks slowly through Boot Hill past the iron restraints embedded in trees, whistling 'I Love the Lord' for courage as leaves spiral in the wind

scenes.heading: 134 INT. NICKEL ACADEMY - SCHOOL HALLWAY - 1967 - DAY (D66) 134 134
scenes.summary: In black and white, Turner encounters a skinny mule with straw in its mouth in the school hallway, touching it to confirm it's real as it looks at him

scenes.heading: 135 INT. NICKEL ACADEMY - DORMITORY - 1967 - DUSK (D66) 135 135
scenes.summary: Jaime rushes from the white campus to warn Turner that 'they will take him tonight' - Elwood has been captured and is being held in the attic sweatbox
scenes.turningPointNote: Elwood's capture sets up the final confrontation with Nickel's punishment system

scenes.heading: 136 INT. NICKEL - ATTIC SWEAT BOX - 1967 - DUSK (D66) 136 136
scenes.summary: Elwood suffers in the darkness of the attic sweatbox as vibrations and screaming sounds build to a roar like a passenger jet falling toward Earth

scenes.heading: 137 OMITTED 137 137
scenes.summary: Omitted scene

scenes.heading: 138 ARCHIVAL FOOTAGE 138 138
scenes.summary: Archival footage shows mules and men working to topple and uproot a colossal Southern tree, symbolizing the destruction of entrenched systems

scenes.heading: 139 INT. NICKEL ACADEMY - SWEAT BOX - 1967 - NIGHT (N66) 139 139
scenes.summary: Turner finds Elwood after days in the sweat box, his head barely able to lift as he emerges from the darkness into the light, weakened and stiff from confinement.
scenes.turningPointNote: The rescue begins - Turner risks everything to free Elwood from certain death

scenes.heading: 140 INT. NICKEL - HALLWAY - 1967 - NIGHT (N66) 140 140
scenes.summary: Turner watches as the dehydrated and weakened Elwood struggles to put on his arrival shirt, his body stiff and painful from the brutal confinement.

scenes.heading: 141 INT. NICKEL - ANOTHER HALLWAY - 1967 - NIGHT (N66) 141 141
scenes.summary: The boys creep through Nickel's hallways in their escape clothes, passing Spencer and other guards playing poker, overhearing their casual conversation while sneaking past.

scenes.heading: 142 OMITTED 142 142
scenes.summary: Omitted scene.

scenes.heading: 143 EXT. HARDEE HOUSE - 1967 - NIGHT (N66) 143 143
scenes.summary: At the Hardee house pool, Turner explains they had to escape tonight because Spencer and others were planning to 'take Elwood out back' - meaning kill him.
scenes.turningPointNote: Reveals the immediate mortal threat that forced the escape attempt

scenes.heading: 144 EXT. COUNTRY ROAD - NEAR MARIANNA - DAWN INTO DAY (D67) 144 144
scenes.summary: The boys ride bicycles through dawn on a country road, Elwood thanking Turner for the rescue, when a red pickup truck passes them in the growing daylight.

scenes.heading: EXT. DIRT ROAD - CANES FIELDS - MORNING (D67) 144A
scenes.summary: Turner rides ahead through sunny cane fields, laughing joyfully at their freedom until he spots the Community Service Van with headlights racing toward Elwood behind him.
scenes.turningPointNote: The moment their escape is discovered and pursuit begins

scenes.heading: EXT. FURROWED CANE FIELD - MORNING [CONTINUOUS] (D67) 144B
scenes.summary: Both boys abandon their bikes and run into a scorched cane field as the van stops and Harper chases with a rifle, firing shots as they flee toward the woods.

scenes.heading: 145 OMITTED 145 145
scenes.summary: Archival footage of prison escapes with bloodhounds, white men on horses, and armed pursuits through swampy forests.

scenes.heading: EXT. RURAL LANDSCAPE - FLORIDA - DUSK (D67) 145B
scenes.summary: Turner runs alone through grassy landscape at dusk, continuing his flight. More archival footage shows the historical context of such manhunts with dogs and aircraft.

scenes.heading: 146 OMITTED 146 146
scenes.summary: Omitted scene.

scenes.heading: 147 EXT. RURAL LANDSCAPE - FLORIDA - NIGHT (N67) 147 147
scenes.summary: Turner continues running alone through the darkness of night, slowing and starting again, pushing himself beyond his limits.

scenes.heading: 148 OMITTED 148 148
scenes.summary: Omitted scene.

scenes.heading: 149 OMITTED 149 149
scenes.summary: A montage of archival images including space shuttles, brain scans, industrial machinery, electric chair viewing rooms, and Black families, creating a stream of consciousness.

scenes.heading: EXT. CURTIS HOME - FRENCHTOWN FL - 1967 -DAY (D-FF8) W150
scenes.summary: Hattie opens her door to Turner, immediately understanding from his presence that Elwood is dead, backing away trembling as she receives the devastating news.
scenes.turningPointNote: The revelation of Elwood's death and its impact on his grandmother

scenes.heading: INT. ADULT ELWOOD’S APARTMENT - 2002 - NYC
scenes.summary: Adult Turner in 2002 sits with his wife Millie in their NYC apartment as she films him with a new digital camcorder, showing a peaceful domestic life.

scenes.heading: INT. ADULT ELWOOD'S APARTMENT - 2002 - NYC
scenes.summary: Millie films Turner sleeping, they laugh together looking at themselves in the camcorder monitor, sharing intimate moments. A death notice reveals Hattie died in 1998.
scenes.turningPointNote: Shows Turner living Elwood's life, having taken his identity

scenes.heading: EXT. NICKEL GROUNDS - 1967 - DAY YY150
scenes.summary: Turner's POV looks up at tree canopy from an open grave as Elwood appears above, laughs, and reaches down to pull Turner up and out to safety.
scenes.turningPointNote: Final revelation that Turner died and Elwood survived, recontextualizing the entire narrative
